1992.tagline: Earthquakes shook the heart of Europe
narrative: On February 13, 1992, a magnitude 5.1 earthquake struck northern Belgium, causing widespread damage and disruption.
The epicenter of the quake was located near the town of Roermond, Netherlands, but the tremors were felt across the border in Belgium.
Miraculously, no lives were lost, but the estimated damage was significant, amounting to $223,558,000 USD, highlighting the importance of disaster preparedness and response.
